title: bossiaea rupicola
intro: Bossiaea rupicola, also known as the rock bossiaea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been traditionally used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Bossiaea rupicola include its ability to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and it has also been used to treat fever and pain. In herbal preparations, Bossiaea rupicola is often made into a decoction or infusion, and can be used as a topical application or ingested as a tea.
